pi_items.source: File verified to exist at benchmarks/cua_world/environments/openbci_gui_env/data/OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt with 15,258 rows, 250 Hz sample rate, 8 channels

OpenBCI documentation and community forums confirm 60Hz notch filter is standard frequency for North America (US); Verified at https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/ and OpenBCI forums

OpenBCI documentation confirms 1-50Hz is one of the standard included bandpass filter options in the GUI; mentioned as common filter range in https://neurobb.com/t/openbci-why-are-1-50hz-bandpass-and-60hz-notch-filters-both-applied-by-default/23

Stream name is user-customizable in OpenBCI GUI Networking widget; no documentation found indicating this is a standard/predefined name, but custom names are supported by LSL protocol. Verified as plausible custom name but not from official sources.
Directory structure verified to be created by setup_openbci.sh and setup_task.sh scripts; path is consistent with OpenBCI GUI default installation locations

Data file header explicitly states: %Source = PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0); Verified at https://www.physionet.org/content/eegmmidb/1.0.0/ - real public scientific dataset from MIT Laboratory for Computational Physiology
Data file header confirms 250 Hz sample rate; OpenBCI Cyton board specification confirmed at https://docs.openbci.com/Cyton/CytonDataFormat/ - default sampling rate is 250 Hz for 8-channel Cyton
Data file header: %Number of channels = 8; OpenBCI Cyton board confirmed as 8-channel at https://shop.openbci.com/products/cyton-biosensing-board-8-channel

pi_items.verified_value: Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2
pi_items.source: Data file header explicitly lists: %Channel Order = Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2; these are standard 10-20 EEG electrode positions
Data file header: %Board = OpenBCI_V3; This refers to the OpenBCI Cyton board, the 8-channel biosensing board

OpenBCI forum and documentation confirm filter on/off button exists in Networking widget for TimeSeries data type; verified at https://openbci.com/forum/index.php?p=/discussion/1190/networking-widget and related discussions about filtered LSL streams
Data file header: %Subject = S001; Consistent with PhysioNet dataset subject numbering

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real EEG data from the PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), specifically subject S001 eyes-open recording with 8 channels (Fp1, Fp2, C3, C4, P7, P8, O1, O2) sampled at 250 Hz from OpenBCI Cyton board. The playback file OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt is verified to exist. The specified filter settings (60Hz notch, 1-50Hz bandpass) are standard in OpenBCI GUI and valid for the North American electrical grid. LSL streaming with filtered data transmission is supported via the Networking widget's filter toggle for TimeSeries data type. The custom stream name 'NeuroPrep_Stream' is user-definable and supported by the LSL protocol.
